tool.name: GROBID
tool.description: extract structured TEI XML from scholarly documents and patents
tool.command:
#if $document.kind == "article_pdf": #set endpoint = "processFulltextDocument" #set input_file = $document.input_pdf #set upload_mode = "multipart" #elif $document.kind == "patent_pdf": #set endpoint = "processCitationPatentPDF" #set input_file = $document.input_pdf #set upload_mode = "multipart" #elif $document.kind == "patent_st36": #set endpoint = "processCitationPatentST36" #set input_file = $document.input_xml #set upload_mode = "multipart" #else: #set endpoint = "processCitationPatentTXT" #set input_file = $document.input_text #set upload_mode = "urlencoded" #end if
bash '$__tool_directory__/run_grobid_service.sh' --endpoint '$endpoint' --upload-mode '$upload_mode' --input '$input_file' --output '$output' --consolidate-header '$advanced.consolidate_header' --consolidate-citations '$advanced.consolidate_citations' $advanced.include_raw_citations $advanced.include_raw_affiliations --start-page '$advanced.start_page' --end-page '$advanced.end_page'

param.name: kind
param.label: Document processing mode
param.option: Patent PDF citation extraction to TEI
Patent ST.36 XML citation extraction to TEI
Patent text citation extraction to TEI

param.name: start_page
param.label: Start page
param.help: Use -1 to start from the first page.

param.name: end_page
param.label: End page
param.help: Use -1 to process to the last page.

tool.help:
**GROBID** starts the GROBID service inside the Galaxy job container and submits one document to the local API.
The tool always returns TEI XML. Scientific article PDFs are sent to ``/api/processFulltextDocument`` for full-text document structuring. Patent inputs are sent to one of the patent citation extraction endpoints and return TEI containing the source text plus extracted patent and non-patent citation structures.
Advanced options expose the main GROBID consolidation and raw-string flags. Page limits only apply to multipart PDF/XML requests; use ``-1`` to leave a bound unset.
The service is started and stopped for each job, so no external GROBID server is required. The launcher writes a job-local GROBID configuration and temporary directory so the container works with Galaxy's non-root Docker execution.
